meta.description: A human-readable string that resolves to a UUID.

targetId.description: The item this alias resolves to. The alias item lives under the alias type-UUID container and associates with its target via this field (the universal payload-dimension pattern); the alias string itself is item.value (case-insensitive lookup, original casing preserved).

assignedBy.description: The entity item that owns/scopes this alias — a user, team, division, or company (all ordinary items). Replaces the old personal|org|type `scope` enum: an alias is visible to a viewer when assignedBy is the viewer or any group/org the viewer belongs to, resolved by walking the ReBAC membership graph (the same graph grants traverse). null = unscoped (visible to all / not yet entity-scoped). On a lookup clash, nearest-in-the-graph wins (personal over org over type). The precise membership relationship-type the traversal follows is settled with the relationships/access model.

provisional.description: true when the alias was computed locally at creation time and has not yet been confirmed as globally unique. false once confirmed at merge time. Manually created aliases are created non-provisional.

confirmedAt.description: Timestamp at which this alias was confirmed unique. null while provisional: true; set to the confirmation time (merge-time uniqueness check for computed aliases, creation time for manual ones) once provisional is false.

computedFromFormulaId.description: If this alias was generated by a formula (e.g. a per-type sequential id like INV-001), the UUID of that `formula` item; null for manually created aliases. Flattened from the former `computedFrom: { formulaId }` object — an inline object cannot be a projected column, so the single ref is carried directly. A type-wide formula alias is simply this field set plus assignedBy = whichever entity owns the formula; there is no separate `type` scope.

sqlSchema: -- Built-in type: adapter handles storage natively.
